rulings.Case Title: 6315 THIRD STREET, LLC VS. GREGORY HAYNES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT LARRY HAYNES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for trespass and ejectment (first and second causes of action) is overruled. All elements of these causes of action are alleged and the allegations are not uncertain. Demurrer to the causes of action of unlawful detainer (third and fourth causes of action) is sustained. Another action is pending for the same causes of action. Plaintiff did not move to consolidate the actions and did not dismiss the unlawful detainer action prior to amending this complaint. Additionally, the third cause of action fails as it is based on a fatally defective notice seeking rent for March to August 2020. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a Notice overstating the amount legally due may support a complaint which purports to "strike" the offending overstated portion of the demand. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PETER DINH VS. ROB MILLS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside Order Granting Defendant Rob Millss Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2021 line 3. PLAINTIFF PETER DINH Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside Order Granting Defendant Rob Millss Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint is DENIED. The motion is made on several grounds (some expressly stated and some not). To the extent the motion is based on extrinsic fraud, the motion is denied. Moving party failed to show any extrinsic fraud, and at most, attempted to show perjury/intrinsic fraud. To the extent the motion is based on CCP 473(b) discretionary relief, the motion is denied as no surprise, mistake or excusable neglect is shown. Furthermore, no proposed alternative opposition brief and supporting evidence to the original ANTI-SLAPP motion is submitted with this motion. To the extent the motion is based on CCP 473(b) mandatory relief, the motion fails to cite any authority for the proposition that such relief is available or what exactly was the attorney's fault that led to default/dismissal. The motion also mentions reconsideration. To the extent this is a motion for reconsideration, it is untimely and there is no reasonable explanation for not locating relevant evidence in possession of the moving party in a timely fashion (or seek an extension for discovery allowable under ANTI-SLAPP statute). Additionally, on the merits, nothing presented in this motion would change the outcome of the ruling on ANTI-SLAPP. The claims in this action (as alleged) arose out of Mr. Mills asking Mr. Dinh to sign a new lease without knowledge whether the new lease was materially the same as the old lease and Mr. Mills seeking to terminate Mr. Dinh's tenancy based on Mr. Dinh's refusal to sign the new lease. The Court found that Mr. Dinh failed to carry his burden under the second prong of ANTI-SLAPP and show probability of prevailing on the merits, in this case, evidence that Mr. Mills knew that 2008 written lease existed, all terms of that lease, and nevertheless asked Mr. Dinhto sign a new inconsistent lease. Mr. Dinh still presents no such evidence. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
